questions.question: A circle has center (3, −2) and passes through the point (7, 1). Write the equation of the circle.
questions.explanation: Find the radius using the distance formula: r = √[(7−3)² + (1−(−2))²] = √[16 + 9] = 5. The standard form is (x − h)² + (y − k)² = r².

questions.question: A sector of a circle has radius 6 and central angle 60°. What is the area of the sector?
The circle x² + y² − 4x + 6y − 12 = 0 has center (h, k) and radius r. Find h + k + r.
questions.explanation: Complete the square: (x² − 4x + 4) + (y² + 6y + 9) = 12 + 4 + 9 = 25. Center is (2, −3), radius is 5. So h + k + r = 2 + (−3) + 5 = 4.

questions.question: A line is tangent to the circle (x + 1)² + (y − 3)² = 16 at the point (2, 3). What is the slope of the tangent line?
questions.explanation: The radius from center (−1, 3) to point (2, 3) has slope 0 (horizontal line). The tangent is perpendicular to the radius, so an undefined slope becomes slope 0 (horizontal tangent).

questions.question: An arc on a circle with radius 8 has length 4π. Find the central angle in degrees.
A circle has equation x² + y² = 49. A chord of the circle has endpoints at (7, 0) and (0, 7). What is the length of the chord?
questions.explanation: Use the distance formula between (7, 0) and (0, 7): d = √[(7−0)² + (0−7)²] = √[49 + 49] = √98 = 7√2.
